category_sources: Official OpenFGA documentation describes OpenFGA as an open source authorization system for developers.
source_facts.description: High performance and flexible authorization/permission engine
display_name_sources: Official website and docs use OpenFGA.
docs_sources: Official GitHub README points users to OpenFGA documentation for production and configuration details.
Official OpenFGA docs site provides getting started, modeling, configuration, and production guidance.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo already contains https://github.com/openfga/openfga, so repo is returned as null per instruction.
tags_sources: Official GitHub README topics include security, permissions, authorization, RBAC, ReBAC, Zanzibar, and fine-grained authorization.
Official OpenFGA documentation mentions Relationship-Based Access Control, RBAC, ABAC scenarios, SDKs, HTTP and gRPC APIs, and CLI tooling.
